sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Kosmos Energy Gains Momentum as Analysts Upgrade Stock Ratings
feed.summary: Kosmos Energy Ltd. (NYSE: KOS) saw its stock price rise by 7.81% due to increasing investor confidence, strategic expansions, and favorable market trends. Analysts, including Johnson Rice, have upgraded their ratings and price targets, indicating optimism for the company's future despite past financial fluctuations. The company's resilience in evolving energy landscapes and recent internal stock purchases by its CFO suggest a promising outlook, though market patience is advised to validate this optimism.

feed.title: Capital One’s Dean Lia sells $314k in stock
feed.summary: Dean Lia, President of Banking & Premium Products at Capital One Financial, sold 1,692 shares of company stock for a total of $314,052. This transaction occurred on April 1, 2026, under a pre-arranged trading plan. The article also touches upon recent analyst ratings and the company's executive compensation plans, including CEO Richard D. Fairbank's $40 million pay package for 2025.

feed.title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN): Billionaire Steve Cohen Is Buying This Tech Stock
feed.summary: Billionaire Steve Cohen's Point72 Asset Management has significantly increased its stake in Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), now holding 5.2 million shares. This move highlights Amazon's strong performance, particularly its Amazon Web Services (AWS) division, which is experiencing a growth resurgence driven by AI and proprietary chips. The article suggests that while Amazon is a strong investment, other AI stocks might offer even higher, quicker returns.

feed.title: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA): Billionaire Steve Cohen Has a Long-Term Bet on This Stock
feed.summary: Billionaire Steve Cohen's Point72 Asset Management has significantly increased its long-term bet on NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA), holding nearly 10 million shares by the end of Q4 2025. This move highlights hedge fund confidence in NVIDIA's role in the AI supercycle, especially as the company projects massive sales for its Blackwell and upcoming Vera Rubin chip systems. The article notes a shift in focus towards AI inference and critical efficiency gains as reasons for this investment, while also suggesting other AI stocks might offer greater upside.

feed.title: Capital One (COF) banking president sells 1,692 shares via 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Dean Lia, President of Banking & Premium Products at Capital One Financial (COF), sold 1,692 shares of common stock at $185.61 per share through a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on August 14, 2025. Following this transaction, Lia continues to hold 65,454 shares of Capital One common stock. This sale, valued at $314,052, was reported in an SEC Form 4 filing, indicating it was scheduled in advance rather than being discretionarily timed.

feed.title: [Form 4] CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORP Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Celia Karam, President of the Retail Bank at CAPITAL ONE FINANCIAL CORP (COF), reported an open-market sale of 1,099 shares of common stock on April 1, 2026, at an average price of $185.61 per share. This transaction was executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on August 14, 2025. Following the sale, Karam still holds 63,323 shares, indicating that this was a small, routine disposition rather than a significant change in her equity stake or a strong directional signal.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) grants 28,625 RSUs to general counsel Kerry Acocella
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc. (DDOG) has granted 28,625 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) of Class A Common Stock to its General Counsel and Secretary, Kerry Acocella. The grant, with a purchase price of $0.00 per share, increases Acocella's direct holdings to 154,346 shares. The RSUs will vest in installments starting March 1, 2027, contingent on her continuous service with the company.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) Chief People Officer granted 25,354 RSUs
feed.summary: Datadog's Chief People Officer, David Galloreese, received a grant of 25,354 restricted stock units (RSUs) of Class A common stock at no cash cost. This grant, detailed in a Form 4 SEC filing, increases his direct holdings to 130,886 shares following the award. The RSUs will vest over time, with the first portion on March 1, 2027, and the remainder in quarterly installments, contingent on his continuous service.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) director sells 20,000 shares in Rule 10b5-1 trade
feed.summary: Datadog director Amit Agarwal reported the sale of 20,000 Class A Common Stock shares on April 1, 2026, at $120.53 per share, totaling $2.41 million. This transaction stemmed from a conversion of Class B shares and was executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on December 8, 2025. Following the sale, his family trust retains significant indirect holdings in both Class A and Class B shares.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) CEO receives 130,858 RSUs vesting from 2027
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc.'s CEO, Olivier Pomel, has been granted 130,858 restricted stock units (RSUs) of Class A Common Stock. This compensation award will bring his directly held Class A Common Stock position to 835,679 shares. The RSUs will begin vesting on March 1, 2027, with 12/48 of the shares vesting then, followed by 12 equal quarterly installments, contingent on his continuous service with the company.

feed.title: Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) awards 54,797 RSUs to Chief Revenue Officer
feed.summary: Datadog's Chief Revenue Officer, Sean Michael Walters, was granted 54,797 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) as a compensation award, not an open-market purchase. These RSUs vest over time, with the first portion vesting on March 1, 2027, and subsequent installments quarterly, contingent on his continued service. Following this grant, Walters directly holds 293,181 shares of Class A Common Stock.

feed.title: Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) CTO receives 94,054 RSUs, holds 531,480 shares
feed.summary: Datadog's Chief Technology Officer, Alexis Le-Quoc, was granted 94,054 restricted stock units (RSUs) as part of his compensation, not an open-market purchase. These RSUs will vest over time, starting March 1, 2027, contingent on his continuous service with the company. Following this grant, Le-Quoc directly holds 531,311 Class A Common Stock shares, with an additional 169 shares held indirectly through a revocable trust.

feed.title: Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) director gets equity grant in lieu of cash
feed.summary: Datadog director Dominic Phillips received a grant of 40 shares of Class A Common Stock, valued at $118.05 per share, as compensation in lieu of cash fees. This transaction, detailed in a Form 4 filing, increases his direct holdings to 5,191 Class A shares. The equity grant was issued under the company’s non-employee director compensation policy.

feed.title: [Form 4] Datadog, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc.'s Chief Financial Officer, David M. Obstler, reported an acquisition of 59,295 shares of Class A Common Stock through restricted stock units (RSUs). These RSUs vest over time, starting March 1, 2027, and are contingent on continuous service. Following this grant, Obstler holds 525,914 shares directly.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) COO Adam Blitzer receives 59,295 RSU stock award
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc. COO Adam Blitzer has been awarded 59,295 shares of Class A Common Stock in restricted stock units (RSUs). These RSUs carry no purchase price and will increase his direct holdings to 289,615 shares once fully vested. The vesting schedule begins on March 1, 2027, with subsequent installments quarterly, provided Blitzer remains in continuous service with the company.

feed.title: Datadog (DDOG) Chief Product Officer receives 51,116-share RSU grant
feed.summary: Datadog, Inc.'s Chief Product Officer, Li Yanbing, was granted 51,116 restricted stock units (RSUs) of Class A Common Stock. These RSUs will vest over time, with the first portion vesting on March 1, 2027, provided Li remains in continuous service with the company. Following this grant, Li Yanbing directly holds 305,117 shares of Class A Common Stock.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ: ULTA) seeks votes on directors, officer exculpation, forum clause
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty is seeking shareholder votes on several key matters at its virtual annual meeting on June 9, 2026. These proposals include the election of ten directors, approval of Certificate of Incorporation amendments for officer exculpation and a forum selection clause, ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as auditor, approval of executive compensation, and the 2026 Incentive Award Plan. The proxy statement details the company's governance practices, director compensation, executive compensation structure, and a new severance plan, highlighting a focus on aligning management and shareholder interests.

feed.title: Vertex (VRTX) legal chief sells 978 shares under 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Joy Liu, EVP and Chief Legal Officer of Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX), sold 978 shares of common stock at an average price of $449.17 per share on April 1, 2026. This transaction was executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on November 25, 2025. Following the sale, Liu directly holds 21,833 Vertex shares.

feed.title: HPE (NYSE: HPE) director converts 14,500 RSUs into common shares
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise director Pamela L. Carter converted 14,500 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common shares. These RSUs, initially granted on May 2, 2025, and including dividend equivalent rights, vested at the company's 2026 Annual Stockholders Meeting. Carter elected to defer the actual receipt of these 14,500 common shares until her service on the Board of Directors concludes.

feed.title: Director James F. O'Neil receives 837 phantom stock units at FirstEnergy (NYSE: FE)
feed.summary: FirstEnergy director James F. O'Neil was granted 837 Phantom Stock Units on April 1, 2026, as part of his quarterly director compensation under the company's 2020 Incentive Compensation Plan. These units, which have no exercise price and are economically equivalent to common shares, are payable in cash or shares after his service concludes. Following this award, O'Neil now holds 45,814.8266 Phantom Stock Units and 1,869 shares of common stock directly.

feed.title: [Form 4] INTUIT INC. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: INTUIT INC. EVP, People and Places, Caryl Lyn Hilliard, exercised 469 restricted stock units into common stock on April 1, 2026, as part of her equity compensation. Following these transactions, her direct holdings increased to 23,152.915 shares, including shares acquired through an Employee Stock Purchase Plan. A portion of shares, 212.093, were disposed of at $432.38 per share to cover tax obligations.

feed.title: Collegium (COLL) EVP has 8,840 shares withheld for RSU tax payment
feed.summary: Collegium Pharmaceutical EVP David Dieter reported a routine share disposition where 8,840 shares of common stock were withheld at $35.72 per share to cover tax obligations upon the vesting of restricted stock units. This non-market transaction resulted in Dieter directly holding 68,231 shares of Collegium Pharmaceutical common stock. The SEC Form 4 filing clarified that this was a tax-withholding disposition, not an open-market sale.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) director converts 14,500 RSUs into common stock
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) director Raymond E. Ozzie converted 14,500 restricted stock units (RSUs) into common shares on April 1, 2026. This transaction increased his direct holdings to 176,400 common shares, with a reference price of $23.98 per share. The RSUs originated from a May 2, 2025 grant and cliff vested at the 2026 Annual Stockholders Meeting, including additional shares from dividend equivalent rights.

feed.title: FirstEnergy (NYSE: FE) director receives 837 phantom stock units as deferred pay
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Corp. director Paul J. Kaleta was granted 837 Phantom Stock Units on April 1, 2026, as deferred compensation under the company's 2020 Incentive Compensation Plan and Deferred Compensation Plan for Outside Directors. These units, which are the economic equivalent of one share of common stock, will be paid in cash or shares after his board service ends. Following this transaction, Kaleta holds a total of 18,290.4465 Phantom Stock Units and 2,051 shares of common stock directly, reflecting routine, non-market-based compensation.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Fuel Hedge Reshapes Cruise Line Cost And Margin Story
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Cruises (NYSE:RCL) has hedged approximately 60% of its 2026 fuel requirements, providing a buffer against rising crude oil prices, unlike its competitors Carnival and Norwegian Cruise Line. This strategy offers Royal Caribbean more cost predictability and enhances its risk profile, complementing its existing loyalty programs and onboard spending initiatives. Investors should monitor how this hedging strategy impacts margins, management's future guidance on fuel expenses, and how it compares to competitors' approaches.

feed.title: Cencora Expands Retina Network As CFO Retirement Opens New Chapter
feed.summary: Cencora (NYSE:COR) is expanding its specialist healthcare offerings by acquiring the retina division of EyeSouth Partners, integrating it into its Retina Consultants of America network. This strategic move aims to strengthen its position in higher-complexity eye care services. Concurrently, CFO James F. Cleary announced his retirement, effective June 30, 2026, marking a leadership transition that investors will watch closely regarding capital allocation and financial strategy.

feed.title: Agilent Announces Transition Plan for Chief Legal Officer
feed.summary: Agilent Technologies announced a transition plan for its Chief Legal Officer, Bret DiMarco, who will step down from his current roles by September 30, 2026, or earlier if a successor is appointed. DiMarco will then serve as a Special Advisor until December 1, 2026, with a reduced salary but continued benefits and equity vesting, followed by a lump-sum exit payment. This structured approach aims to ensure a smooth leadership transition and manage executive compensation.

feed.title: FirstEnergy (FE) director Hicks granted 837 phantom stock units as deferred pay
feed.summary: FirstEnergy (FE) director Lisa Winston Hicks was granted 837 phantom stock units as part of her deferred compensation under the company's 2020 Incentive Compensation Plan. These units are economically equivalent to common stock and increase her total phantom stock holdings to 18,290.4465 units, payable in cash or shares after her board service ends. The Form 4 filing also reveals her direct holding of 2,051 common shares and an indirect holding of 500 shares through her spouse, with no open-market purchases or sales reported.

feed.title: FirstEnergy (FE) director granted 837 phantom stock units as quarterly board compensation
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Corp. director Steven J. Demetriou received 837 phantom stock units as routine quarterly compensation on April 1, 2026. These units, which mirror the value of FirstEnergy common stock, are deferred into a compensation plan for outside directors and are payable in cash or shares after his board service concludes. Following this grant, Demetriou's holdings increased to 29,555.3933 phantom stock units, in addition to his 12,431 directly held common shares.

feed.title: Intuit (INTU) CFO Aujla exercises RSUs as 1,725 shares withheld for tax
feed.summary: Intuit's EVP and CFO Sandeep Aujla exercised Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) on April 1, 2026, which converted into 3,360 shares of Intuit common stock at a $0.00 exercise price. To cover tax obligations from this vesting, 1,725.362 shares were withheld at a fair market value of $432.38 per share. Following these transactions, Aujla directly holds 2,208.7746 Intuit shares, including those from an Employee Stock Purchase Plan.

feed.title: Special Master Sought In Ex-Commanders Partner's BofA Suit
feed.summary: A Florida federal magistrate judge plans to appoint a special master to review documents withheld by a former Washington Commanders minority owner in his dispute with Bank of America. This decision comes after the judge's initial review revealed that some of the withheld documents were not privileged. The article highlights the ongoing legal battle and the need for an independent review of the information.

feed.title: DEP Invites Comments On Erosion & Sedimentation Permit For Hyperion Midstream [EQT] Pipeline Between The Selene And Hephaestus Shale Gas Well Pads In Upper Burrell Twp., Westmoreland County
feed.summary: The Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) is inviting public comments on an Erosion and Sedimentation Permit for the Hyperion Midstream LLC (EQT Corp) pipeline. This pipeline will connect the Selene and Hephaestus shale gas well pads in Upper Burrell Township, Westmoreland County, and will impact Pucketa Creek. The deadline for comments is May 4, and no hearing has been scheduled yet.

feed.title: Newmont EVP Toth sells $339k in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Newmont Corp's EVP, Chief Sustainability & Development Officer, Peter Toth, sold 3,000 shares of company stock for $339,270. This sale was conducted under a pre-arranged trading plan, yet InvestingPro analysis indicates the stock remains undervalued with strong financial health. The broader gold mining sector faces challenges due to declining gold prices, influenced by robust U.S. labor data reducing expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts.

feed.title: FirstEnergy (NYSE: FE) director awarded 837 phantom stock units as deferred pay
feed.summary: FirstEnergy director Jana T. Croom was granted 837 Phantom Stock Units as deferred compensation on April 1, 2026. These units, which are tied to the value of FirstEnergy common stock, will be paid in cash or shares after her directorship ends. Following this grant, Croom now holds 13,970.2234 phantom stock units and 2,778.521 shares of common stock, including those acquired through dividend reinvestments.

feed.title: ClearOne keeps Derek L. Graham as CEO on transitional consulting basis
feed.summary: ClearOne (CLRO) has retained Derek L. Graham as Chief Executive Officer on a transitional, consulting basis after his employment agreement expired. He will provide consulting services for up to 10 hours per week at $160/hour, continuing to perform all CEO functions. This arrangement has no fixed term and can be terminated by either party.

feed.title: [Form 4] TRACTOR SUPPLY CO /DE/ Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Tractor Supply (TSCO) director Margaret M. Ham received 289.735 shares of common stock at $45.30 per share through the company’s Directors Stock Election Plan. This transaction was a grant in lieu of cash compensation, not an open-market purchase, and increased her direct holdings to 13,302.669 shares. The filing clarifies that this is a routine compensation-related event, aligning her interests with company equity performance.

feed.title: Cadiz Inc (CDZI) director granted 927 shares in lieu of cash fees
feed.summary: Cadiz Inc. (CDZI) director Barbara A. Lloyd received a grant of 927 shares of common stock, valued at $5.055 per share, as compensation for her board services. This stock grant was issued in lieu of cash fees for the three-month period beginning April 1, 2026, under the company’s 2019 Equity Incentive Plan. Following this transaction, Lloyd directly holds 22,272 shares of Cadiz Inc. common stock.

feed.title: [Form 4] CDW Corp Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: CDW Corp director David W. Nelms reported an acquisition of 236 fully vested restricted stock units as equity compensation, granted in lieu of cash for his annual director retainer. This transaction, dated April 1, 2026, increased his direct holdings to 33,025.12 CDW shares, including an adjustment for previously omitted dividend equivalent awards. Settlement of these units into common shares has been deferred.

feed.title: ClearOne (NASDAQ: CLRO) keeps Derek Graham as transitional CEO consultant
feed.summary: ClearOne, Inc. has retained Derek L. Graham as CEO on a transitional basis through a Letter Agreement signed on April 1, 2026, following the expiration of his previous employment contract. Under this new arrangement, Mr. Graham will provide consulting services for up to ten hours per week at $160 per hour, continuing to perform all CEO functions. The agreement allows for termination by either party at any time, ensuring leadership continuity flexibly.

feed.title: Newmont (NEM) EVP Peter Toth sells 3,000 shares under Rule 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Peter Toth, EVP and Chief Sustainability & Development Officer at Newmont (NEM), sold 3,000 shares of common stock at $113.09 per share on April 1, 2026. This transaction was conducted under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on December 17, 2025. Following the sale, Toth directly holds 52,315 shares of Newmont common stock.

feed.title: Fauber, Moody’s CEO, sells $382,662 in MCO stock
feed.summary: Robert Fauber, President and CEO of Moody’s Corp (MCO), sold 875 shares of company stock for a total of $382,662, executed under a pre-arranged trading plan. Concurrently, he exercised options to acquire 1,167 shares. This insider activity follows Moody's reporting strong fourth-quarter earnings and launching a new platform, amidst varied analyst ratings concerning its valuation.

feed.title: Fertitta-linked entity writes calls on 400K Wynn Resorts (WYNN) shares
feed.summary: Entities associated with Tilman Fertitta, including Hospitality Headquarters, Inc., have reported selling call options for 400,000 shares of Wynn Resorts common stock. These options have strike prices of $120 and $122, expire on October 2, 2026, and generated premiums of approximately $4.37 and $3.90 per share. The move is viewed as a hedging or monetization strategy, capping potential upside for the entities above the strike prices while providing immediate income from the premiums.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) CFO sells 5,000 shares under 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks CFO Dipak Golechha sold 5,000 shares of common stock in open-market transactions on April 1, 2026, at prices ranging from $157.81 to $160.70 per share. These sales were conducted under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on December 5, 2025, rather than in response to immediate market events. After these transactions, Golechha directly holds 150,250 shares of Palo Alto Networks common stock.

feed.title: Omnicom (NYSE: OMC) director receives 680-share stock award under 2026 plan
feed.summary: Omnicom (NYSE: OMC) director Patricia Salas Pineda was granted 680.52 shares of common stock as compensation under the Omnicom Group Inc. 2026 Incentive Award Plan. She elected to defer receipt of these shares, increasing her directly held position to 10,540.10 shares, which includes reinvested dividends from deferred shares credited on January 9, 2026. This transaction was an award, not an open-market purchase, and was reported as a neutral filing with neutral sentiment.

feed.title: Omnicom Group (OMC) director granted 680 deferred shares under 2026 incentive plan
feed.summary: Omnicom Group Inc. director Leonard S. Coleman Jr. was granted 680.52 shares of common stock as a compensation award under the company's 2026 Incentive Award Plan. Mr. Coleman elected to defer the receipt of these shares, and his direct holdings now total 49,286.74 shares, including reinvested dividends. This transaction, filed on Form 4, reflects equity compensation rather than a market purchase or sale.

feed.title: Director defers Omnicom (NYSE: OMC) stock awards into 2026 plan
feed.summary: Omnicom Group Inc. director Mark D. Gerstein received two stock awards of the company's common stock on April 1, 2026, totaling 979.29 shares, as part of his board compensation. The awards were granted under the 2026 Incentive Award Plan, and he elected to defer their receipt, including a quarterly retainer payment and dividends reinvested in company stock. Following these grants, Gerstein directly holds 15,167.56 shares of Omnicom common stock.

feed.title: [Form 4] OMNICOM GROUP INC. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Wyatt E. Lee, a director at OMNICOM GROUP INC. (OMC), reported an acquisition of 681 shares of common stock at no purchase price on April 1, 2026. This transaction, detailed in a Form 4 filing, increases his direct holdings to 23,797 shares. The filing also notes that 212 shares were previously omitted from a January 5, 2026 Form 4.

feed.title: [Form 4] OMNICOM GROUP INC. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: OMNICOM GROUP INC. director Linda Johnson Rice received a stock award of 681 shares of common stock at no cost, which was reported on a Form 4. This compensation-related acquisition increased her direct holdings to 12,287.76 shares. The shares include reinvested dividends from deferred shares credited on January 9, 2026.

feed.title: NYSE flags C1 Fund late annual report, listing unchanged
feed.summary: C1 Fund Inc. (NYSE: CFND) announced its inability to file its Annual Report on Form N-CSR for the period ended December 31, 2025, within the prescribed timeframe. The New York Stock Exchange has issued a notice regarding the late filing, though it has no immediate impact on the listing of the Fund's common stock. The delay is attributed to the need for additional time to finalize valuation and accounting documentation for investments, and for its independent auditors to complete their audit.

feed.title: Bank of Hawai‘i sets April 20 earnings call, preferred payouts due May 1
feed.summary: Bank of Hawai‘i Corporation will release its first-quarter 2026 financial results on April 20, 2026, and will host a conference call on the same day. The company's Board of Directors also declared quarterly dividend payments for its Fixed Rate Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock Series A and Series B, payable on May 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 16, 2026.
